controls.title: Broken Access Control
controls.statement: Restrictions on authenticated users are not properly enforced, allowing unauthorized access to data or functions.

controls.title: Cryptographic Failures
controls.statement: Sensitive data is exposed due to weak, missing, or misused cryptography.

controls.title: Injection
controls.statement: Untrusted input is interpreted as a command or query (SQL, OS, LDAP, etc.), including cross-site scripting.

controls.title: Insecure Design
controls.statement: Missing or ineffective security controls by design, not merely implementation bugs.

controls.title: Security Misconfiguration
controls.statement: Insecure defaults, incomplete setup, or overly verbose errors expose the application.

controls.title: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
controls.statement: Use of components with known vulnerabilities or that are unsupported/out of date.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication Failures
controls.statement: Weaknesses in confirming user identity, session management, or credential handling.

controls.title: Software and Data Integrity Failures
controls.statement: Code and infrastructure that does not protect against integrity violations, e.g. insecure deserialization or unverified updates.

controls.title: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
controls.statement: Insufficient logging and monitoring delays breach detection and response.

controls.title: Server-Side Request Forgery
controls.statement: The server fetches a user-supplied URL without validating it, enabling SSRF.
